features

the modern toolkit for teams that actually ship

everything you need to design, prototype, and hand off — without the context switching tax.

browse docs start free trial

real-time collaboration

work alongside teammates on the same board with multi-cursor presence and live typing indicators.

learn more

transparent billing

flat per-seat pricing with no hidden overages. cancel anytime, no retention questions.

learn more

soc 2 + gdpr compliant

independently audited controls, eu data residency options, and per-workspace encryption keys.

learn more
built for scale

six reasons engineering teams switch

every feature is built to unblock the people who ship the work.

api-first architecture

rest + graphql + webhooks. every dashboard action has a documented programmatic equivalent.

version history

snapshot any change, branch a workspace, restore to any previous state without asking support.

design tokens built in

export colour, type, and spacing as css variables, tailwind config, or style dictionary json.

inline commentary

thread discussion against any component or prop, not just the whole file. resolve and keep moving.

release calendar

every component ships with a change-log entry tied to a git commit and a figma version stamp.

localised out of the box

22 locales ship in the base library, with format helpers for currency, date, and measurement.

primary capabilities

a platform, not a plugin

primary

ship design systems, not screenshots

a single source of truth for tokens, components, and patterns — synced from figma into code on every save.

global search

find any component in 80 ms.

library packs

600 presets, opinionated defaults.

asset pipeline

auto-export png, webp, avif at 1x/2x/3x with zero config.

theme engine

light, dark, high-contrast, and any brand palette — from a single token file.

how it works

three steps to a shared language

1

connect your figma library

one-click oauth pulls every component, variant, and token into a project.

2

map to your stack

react, vue, svelte, swiftui — pick an output and we generate the templates.

3

ship with sync turned on

every figma publish opens a pr. review, merge, done.

4,200+

design teams in production

99.98%

api uptime last 12 months

80ms

p95 search latency

600+

components in the pack

workflow

review cycles that don't steal your afternoon

pull requests annotated with component diffs, not screenshots. reviewers see what changed at the token level.

  • token-level diff in every pr description.
  • in-ide preview that matches production css exactly.
  • sync-back: designers can update tokens from code reviews.
  • no more "looks fine on my machine" regressions.
tokens/brand.css 
- --color-brand-600: #7F56D9;
+ --color-brand-600: #6941C6;
  --color-brand-700: #53389E;
  --radius-lg: 8px;
- --shadow-xs: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.04);
+ --shadow-xs: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.05);
alternatives

why not the usual suspects

the four most common paths teams try before landing here.

storybook-only

component catalogue in isolation

great for eng. invisible to design. tokens still live in a second place.

zeroheight

docs-first system

polished handoff docs. render stays separate from runtime code.

in-house wiki

notion + github wiki combo

cheapest on day one. by month six, nobody trusts any page.

untitled

tokens, components, docs, sync

one source of truth, token diffs on every pr, figma stays the canvas.

in production at
axiom blueprint canopy evergreen glacier lumen meridian

stop syncing screenshots
